message.content:
You are confirmer #1, independent. Confirm a production-hardening fix is BOTH appropriate (correct, no regression, idiomatic) AND complete (for a code change the named test must DISCRIMINATE — fail if reverted; for a doc/decision item, judge whether the documented claim is now accurate/honest). Repo /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay
Fix H-013 in src/assay_engine/pipeline.py
What: provenance-trail artifact tempfile unlinked after tracker copies it (was leaked per run).
Test(s): test_tracker_receives_run_and_metrics_and_failures_dont_abort (asserts temp gone)
Read the actual changed file(s); read the test; check no regression. Verdict CONFIRMED (both true) or CONCERN. Be skeptical and specific.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

011942b fix(hardening-p5): batch B — reliability, CI, observability, docs (13 findings)
a9f3a48 fix(hardening-p5): batch A — methodology integrity / pre-registration firewall (H-001,002,003,015,016)
b483694 docs(hardening): pass-4 merged (#162); open pass-5 with retrospective dimensions
af29d19 Merge pull request #162 from thehipsterciso/harden/pass-4
1a61b89 docs(hardening): record pass-4 (23/24 confirmed, all fixed) + pass-5 dimensions
f5a350f fix(hardening-p4): remediate the 1 confirmation CONCERN (G-004 test discrimination)
7dccfd8 fix(hardening-p4): batch E — egress, error contracts, PII & test discrimination (8 findings)
c49261e fix(hardening-p4): batch D — CI / supply-chain reproducibility (G-015,016,022)
---
commit 011942ba0d024960245c8ab919fdf86388cff686
Author: [REDACTED] <[REDACTED]>
Date:   Wed Jun 17 01:31:14 2026 -0400

    fix(hardening-p5): batch B — reliability, CI, observability, docs (13 findings)
    
    Pass-5 confirmed findings (2-agent verified after self-refutation; revert-discriminated):
    
    Reliability / error contracts:
    - H-004 (med) malformed reasoning params (temperature/seed) raise the typed
      PermanentReasoningError instead of a raw ValueError/TypeError leaking past run()/run_json.
    - H-021 (low) QdrantVectorStore.query rejects non-positive k and skips returned points
      lacking our 'ref' payload (was an opaque KeyError).
    - H-022 (low) vectorstore int env vars parse via _int_env, naming the bad var instead of an
      opaque import-time "invalid literal for int()".
    - H-013 (low) the provenance-trail artifact tempfile is unlinked after the tracker copies it
      (was delete=False, leaked per tracked run).
    
    CI / supply-chain:
    - H-007 (med) license gate surfaces UNKNOWN/empty-license packages as a ::warning:: (hidden-
      copyleft vector) instead of passing them silently; still fails only on explicit deny matches.
    - H-008 (med) workflow pins GITHUB_TOKEN to least-privilege `permissions: contents: read`.
    - H-009 (med) the integration job verifies the Postgres/Qdrant service containers are
      reachable (pg_isready + /readyz) so the live tests can't silently SKIP and pass vacuously.
    
    Observability / governance:
    - H-011 (med) documented the deliberate recorder-optionality asymmetry between the graph gate
      node and run_study (the runner owns + always records to the trail; the node bridges).
    - H-017 (low) documented that serialized provenance records don't embed keying — the verifier
      supplies the secret out-of-band (key management; verify_records fails closed).
    
    Documentation honesty (assertion-vs-implementation):
    - H-010 (med) stable_seed: "distinct inputs get distinct seeds" corrected to the birthday
      bound of the truncated space (not a uniqueness guarantee).
    - H-012 (low) gatenode "atomically"/"terminal idempotency" claims scoped honestly.
    - H-023 (low) redact_creds "always scrubbed precisely" corrected to "precise when the conn
      string appears verbatim" (reformatted strings fall to the regex backstops).
    - H-018 (med) transcript scrubber redacts the username in the project-dir slug at any length.
    - H-024 (low) the in-process unfreeze test is marked illustrative; the seed-pinned subprocess
      test is the discriminating guard.
    
    Split-verdict (1-of-2) findings H-005/H-006/H-019/H-020 and rejected H-014 are documented in
    PASS-5.md as not-proceeding per the both-must-confirm protocol.
    
    Gates: ruff + ruff format --check + mypy --strict clean; 531 passed, 5 skipped.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <[REDACTED]>

content.text: The fix looks clean. Let me read the full context around the change and find the test.

content.content:
728	        # Correlate the hash-chained trail to the experiment run (#G-009): persist it as a JSON
729	        # artifact on the tracker so an auditor opening the run can retrieve the provenance that
730	        # produced its metrics. Best-effort, like the other tracker calls — never aborts the run.
731	        if tracker is not None and run_id is not None:
732	
733	            def _log_trail(t: ExperimentTracker, rid: str) -> None:
734	                import json
735	                import os
736	                import tempfile
737	
738	                from assay_engine.provenance import entries_to_records
739	
740	                with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(
741	                    "w", suffix="_provenance.json", delete=False, encoding="utf-8"
742	                ) as fh:
743	                    json.dump(list(entries_to_records(list(trail.entries))), fh, indent=2)
744	                    path = fh.name
745	                # Clean up the scratch file after the tracker has copied it into its store, so a
746	                # tracked run does not leak a temp file (with provenance contents) on every call
747	                # (#H-013). The tracker reads/copies synchronously in log_artifact.
748	                try:
749	                    t.log_artifact(rid, path)
750	                finally:
751	                    try:
752	                        os.unlink(path)
753	                    except OSError:
754	                        pass
755	
756	            track(_log_trail)
757	        ok = True
758	    except Exception as exc:
759	        # Record a terminal failure entry BEFORE re-raising so the abort reason is recoverable
760	        # from the (caller-owned) trail, not lost (#109).
761	        last_phase = visited[-1].name if visited else "start"
762	        try:
763	            trail.record(
764	                "run_failed",
765	                f"{type(exc).__name__} during {last_phase}",
766	                error_type=type(exc).__name__,
767	                message=str(exc)[:2000],

424	def test_tracker_receives_run_and_metrics_and_failures_dont_abort(tmp_path):
425	    class FakeTracker:
426	        def __init__(self):
427	            self.calls = []
428	
429	        def start_run(self, name, params):
430	            self.calls.append(("start", name))
431	            return "run-1"
432	
433	        def log_metric(self, run_id, key, value):
434	            self.calls.append(("metric", key, value))
435	
436	        def log_artifact(self, run_id, path):
437	            self.calls.append(("artifact", path))
438	
439	        def end_run(self, run_id, status="FINISHED"):
440	            self.calls.append(("end", run_id, status))
441	
442	    t = FakeTracker()
443	    res = _run(tmp_path, ALL, tracker=t)
444	    assert res.experiment_run_id == "run-1"
445	    assert ("start", "reference-study") in t.calls and ("end", "run-1", "FINISHED") in t.calls
446	    assert any(c[0] == "metric" and c[1] == "alignment_rate" for c in t.calls)
447	    # #F-029: SLO-relevant scale + latency metrics are logged so corpus-size↔latency regressions
448	    # are analysable from the experiment store, not just the provenance trail.
449	    metric_keys = {c[1] for c in t.calls if c[0] == "metric"}
450	    assert {"n_units", "n_claims", "run_duration_s"} <= metric_keys
451	    # #G-009: the hash-chained provenance trail is persisted + correlated to the run as an artifact,
452	    # so an auditor opening the run can retrieve the provenance behind its metrics.
453	    artifacts = [c for c in t.calls if c[0] == "artifact"]
454	    assert artifacts and any(str(c[1]).endswith("_provenance.json") for c in artifacts)
455	    # #H-013: the scratch temp file is cleaned up after the tracker copies it (no leak per run).
456	    import os as _os
457	
458	    trail_paths = [c[1] for c in artifacts if str(c[1]).endswith("_provenance.json")]
459	    assert trail_paths and not any(_os.path.exists(p) for p in trail_paths)
460	
461	    class BoomTracker(FakeTracker):
462	        def log_metric(self, *a):
463	            raise RuntimeError("tracker down")
464	
465	    res2 = _run(tmp_path, ADJUDICATE, tracker=BoomTracker())  # must NOT abort the run
466	    assert res2.scorecard is not None
467	    assert any(e.kind == "tracking_error" for e in res2.provenance)
468	
469	
470	def test_versioner_failure_is_wrapped_as_ingestion_error(tmp_path):
471	    # #F-008: a storage failure in the optional versioner seam must surface as the documented
472	    # IngestionError, not a raw untyped exception the caller cannot distinguish from a firewall
473	    # breach. Pre-fix: versioner.put() was called bare and PermissionError propagated raw.
474	    from assay_engine.pipeline import IngestionError
475	
476	    class BoomVersioner:
477	        def put(self, ref_str):
478	            raise PermissionError("read-only store")
479	
480	        def path_for(self, version):  # pragma: no cover - not reached
481	            raise AssertionError
482	
483	    with pytest.raises(IngestionError, match="data versioner failed"):
484	        _run(tmp_path, DISCOVERY, versioner=BoomVersioner())
485	
486	
487	def test_confirm_held_out_returning_none_raises_firewall_violation(tmp_path):
488	    # #F-021: a confirmer that forgets to `return` yields None; the runner must raise a typed
489	    # FirewallViolation, not die on an opaque AttributeError at verdict.hypothesis_id.
490	    plan = ref.make_plan(ref.write_source(tmp_path / "c.json"), modes=DISCOVERY)
491	    with pytest.raises(FirewallViolation, match="expected a Verdict"):
492	        run_study(replace(plan, confirm_held_out=lambda h, c: None), gate_handler=auto_approve)
493	
494	
495	def test_empty_discover_in_pipeline_raises(tmp_path):
496	    # #F-042: a discover() returning no hypotheses is a vacuous run; the pipeline must fail loud
497	    # rather than complete with zero verdicts (indistinguishable from a broken callable).
498	    plan = ref.make_plan(ref.write_source(tmp_path / "c.json"), modes=DISCOVERY)
499	    with pytest.raises(FirewallViolation, match="no corpus units|no hypotheses|discover"):
500	        run_study(replace(plan, discover=lambda c: []), gate_handler=auto_approve)
501	
502	
503	def test_tracker_start_run_failure_warns_and_continues(tmp_path, caplog):
504	    # #F-028: if start_run fails, run_id stays None and ALL metrics silently no-op. The engine
505	    # must emit a structured warning (and a trail entry) so an operator seeing no MLflow run
506	    # knows why, instead of guessing. The run itself still completes.
507	    import logging
508	
509	    class StartBoomTracker:
510	        def start_run(self, name, params):
511	            raise RuntimeError("tracker unreachable")
512	
513	        def log_metric(self, *a):  # pragma: no cover - never reached (run_id is None)
514	            raise AssertionError
515	
516	        def log_artifact(self, *a):  # pragma: no cover
517	            raise AssertionError
518	
519	        def end_run(self, *a, **k):  # pragma: no cover - skipped when run_id is None
520	            raise AssertionError
521	
522	    with caplog.at_level(logging.WARNING, logger="assay_engine.pipeline"):
523	        res = _run(tmp_path, DISCOVERY, tracker=StartBoomTracker())
524	    assert res.experiment_run_id is None
525	    assert any(e.kind == "tracking_error" for e in res.provenance)
526	    assert any("start_run failed" in r.message for r in caplog.records)
527	
528	
529	def test_run_failed_record_failure_is_logged_not_swallowed(tmp_path, caplog):
530	    # #F-030: if recording the terminal run_failed entry itself raises, the failure reason must
531	    # not vanish silently — a structured warning preserves it in the logs even though it is
532	    # absent from the trail. The original exception is still re-raised.
533	    import logging

content.text: The test captures the artifact path and asserts the file no longer exists. The key question for discrimination: the `FakeTracker.log_artifact` only records the path, it does NOT copy/move the file. So the temp file remains on disk unless the new `os.unlink` runs. Let me confirm the test discriminates by reverting and running.

input.command:
cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && cp src/assay_engine/pipeline.py /tmp/pipeline.bak && python - <<'EOF'
import re
p="src/assay_engine/pipeline.py"
s=open(p).read()
old='''                # Clean up the scratch file after the tracker has copied it into its store, so a
                # tracked run does not leak a temp file (with provenance contents) on every call
                # (#H-013). The tracker reads/copies synchronously in log_artifact.
                try:
                    t.log_artifact(rid, path)
                finally:
                    try:
                        os.unlink(path)
                    except OSError:
                        pass'''
new='''                t.log_artifact(rid, path)'''
assert old in s
s=s.replace(old,new)
open(p,"w").write(s)
print("reverted")
EOF
python -m pytest tests/test_pipeline.py::test_tracker_receives_run_and_metrics_and_failures_dont_abort -q 2>&1 | tail -15

content.text:
Working tree is clean (matches HEAD, fix intact) and the test passes. Discrimination confirmed: reverting to bare `t.log_artifact(rid, path)` made the test FAIL with `AssertionError` on the H-013 line (temp file still existed).

Verification summary:

**Appropriate:**
- The fix wraps `t.log_artifact(rid, path)` in `try/finally` and calls `os.unlink(path)` afterward, catching `OSError` (idempotent cleanup). Correct: `NamedTemporaryFile(delete=False)` requires explicit unlink, and the tracker's contract is to copy synchronously in `log_artifact`, so deleting after the call is safe.
- No regression: the artifact is still logged before deletion (assertions for `_provenance.json` artifact still pass); the `finally` guarantees cleanup even if `log_artifact` raises, and the broader best-effort tracking semantics (`track(...)`) still swallow tracking errors so the run doesn't abort. The `BoomTracker` sub-case still passes.
- `import os` was correctly added inside the closure alongside the existing local imports. The bare-except is narrowed to `OSError` (idiomatic).

**Complete (test discriminates):**
- `tests/test_pipeline.py:455-459` captures the artifact path(s) and asserts none exist post-run. The `FakeTracker.log_artifact` only records the path (does not copy/move), so the temp file persists unless the new unlink runs.
- Revert → 1 failed (`assert ... not any(_os.path.exists(p) ...)` fired); fix → 1 passed. The test fails if reverted.

Relevant paths:
- `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/pipeline.py` (lines 745-754)
- `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/tests/test_pipeline.py` (lines 424-459)

This correctly fixes the leak — NamedTemporaryFile(delete=False) requires explicit removal, and the tracker copies synchronously in log_artifact so deleting after is safe. No regression: the artifact is still logged (artifact assertions still pass), the finally guarantees cleanup even if log_artifact raises, and the outer best-effort track(...) semantics still prevent run abort (BoomTracker sub-case passes). import os correctly added in the closure; except narrowed to OSError (idiomatic).

Complete/discriminating: tests/test_pipeline.py:455-459 captures the *_provenance.json artifact path(s) and asserts none exist after the run. FakeTracker.log_artifact only records the path (no copy/move), so the temp file persists unless the new unlink runs. I reverted the fix to bare `t.log_artifact(rid, path)` and the test FAILED with AssertionError on the H-013 line (file still existed at /var/folders/.../tmp...._provenance.json); with the fix restored it PASSES (1 passed).
